title: Logon Path on iSeries
text: Logon Path on iSeries $DB2 DB2: { DbmsDirectory } : { Database } | { DB2UserName } | { DB2Password } DbmsDirectory —name of the local relational database directory entry Database —collection or schema name in which the data resides. This collection or schema is actually a library that must have been created using the create collection or create schema SQL statement. Libraries that were created using other methods do not contain the DB2 catalog table views required by Uniface, and therefore cannot be used. DB2UserName and DB2Password —user and password for the DB2 database. Optional. Local Relational Database Directory Entry You can obtain the name of the local relational database directory entry by using the CL command WRKRDBDIRE . From the list of entries displayed, use the name of the one that has *LOCAL as its remote location. If there is no such entry, you must create one: In the Option field on the top line, specify a 1 . In the empty Entry field to its right, specify a name. Note: It is common practice to use the network name of the iSeries machine or the machine’s serial number. Click Enter . In the Name or address field, enter *LOCAL . Normal and Server Mode on iSeries On iSeries, a running Uniface Server job or Uniface batch job always has an implicit connection to the database. When you omit the DB2UserName and DB2Password in the path, the connector uses this implicit connection. This is called Normal mode and limits data access to this one connection. The authorities it has for accessing or modifying database objects are those of the user profile under which the job runs. When you specify the DB2UserName and DB2Password , you instruct the connector to establish an explicit connection using the provided user name and password, instead of using the implicit database connection. This is called Server mode , and it allows more than one connection to the database. This is required when components are activated with TRANSACTION=TRUE qualifier. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
